opinion.h1: KIRSCHKE v. CITY OF HOUSTON, 364 U.S. 474 (1960)
opinion.h4: KIRSCHKE ET AL. v. CITY OF HOUSTON. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TEXAS. No. 426. Decided December 5, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed since the judgment below is based on a nonfederal ground adequate to support it.
R. H. Burks and Homer T. Bouldin for appellee.
The appeal herein is dismissed for the reason that the judgment of the Supreme Court of Texas, sought here to be reviewed, is based upon a nonfederal ground adequate to support it.
364 U.S. 474 RIELA v. NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COUNTY COURT OF TIOGA COUNTY, NEW YORK. No. 445. Decided December 5, 1960.
George Boldman and Eliot H. Lumbard for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
THE CHIEF JUSTICE, MR. JUSTICE BLACK and MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
